title: Node Fetch, Redirect Control, Streams, Timeouts, and Bounded Bodies · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for node fetch, redirect control, streams, timeouts, and bounded bodies and accepts only compiler plus runtime changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named network, DNS, browser, accessibility, security, load, legal-review, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for bounded Fetch response adapter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Set honest User-Agent, Accept, conditional validators, safe methods, credentials policy, and manual redirect handling through one adapter.
Classify transport, status, media type, length, encoding, validator, and domain outcome before parsing.
Count compressed, decoded, consumed, and retained bytes during stream processing and cancel over-budget bodies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
